result:
I’d make it impossible for people to dehumanize each other.

Not “be nicer,” not “agree more,” but actually remove that mental switch that lets someone see another person as less real, less worthy, or disposable. A lot of the worst things—war, exploitation, cruelty, even quieter everyday harm—depend on that ability to flatten someone into an object or a label.

If that switch didn’t exist, disagreements would still happen, power struggles too, but there’d be a hard ceiling on how far people would go. You couldn’t casually justify suffering anymore, because it would always feel like it was happening to someone as vivid and real as yourself.

It wouldn’t make the world perfect. But it would cut out a huge amount of unnecessary damage at the root.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
